title: AutoLogon plaintext password stored in registry
description: Fires when the AutoLogon analyzer reports a DefaultPassword value present in Winlogon. A plaintext AutoLogon password stored in the registry is readable by any local administrator and is a credential exposure — more severe than AutoLogon alone (the Sysinternals Autologon tool deliberately avoids this by storing the secret in the LSA instead).
explanation:
This device has a **plaintext AutoLogon password stored in the registry** (`DefaultPassword` under `HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon`). This value is readable by any local administrator and exposes the AutoLogon account's credentials in clear text.

Even when AutoLogon itself is intended (e.g. a kiosk), the password should never be left as a registry value. The Sysinternals Autologon tool stores the secret as an encrypted LSA secret precisely to avoid this; a plaintext `DefaultPassword` usually means a script or manual edit wrote the credentials directly.

Note: the agent only reports the *presence* of this value — it never reads or transmits the password itself.

remediation.title: Remove the plaintext password
steps: Delete the DefaultPassword value under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon
If AutoLogon is still required, reconfigure it with the Sysinternals Autologon tool so the secret is stored as an encrypted LSA secret rather than a registry value
Rotate the exposed account's password, since it may have been readable on disk

remediation.title: Reduce blast radius
steps: Verify the AutoLogon account is the least-privileged account that satisfies the use case (not a domain or local admin where avoidable)
For kiosk scenarios, prefer Assigned Access / kiosk profiles over a raw AutoLogon credential
